reviews.title: Poor Measurements - Support Cannot Help
reviews.text:
After charging for 30 minutes as instructed, I paired the smart watch with the app. This was easy, and the one reason I gave it 2 stars instead of 1. The other reason for 2 stars, the interface was easier to use than I expected.

But if the measurements are not accurate, ease of use doesn't matter.

I bought this smart watch to track my heart rate while exercising—mainly walking. After recent heart procedures, my doctor wants me to keep my pulse below a certain rate. Each time I measure, the pulse differs substantially from the pulse oximeter that always agrees with my doctor's office pulse oximeter, and heart monitor. It goes as high as 90 when the PO says 52. I took multiple measurements, including taking my own pulse. The closest measurements were 60 when the actual rate was 51 or 52.

Support is through a chat on the app. I did not wait long for support, however they did not try to help me. They insisted that the heart rate changes. That I had to let it sync with the app. Not once did they address the possibility that the smart watch might be reading the rate wrong. Told me to try again after I had already tried multiple times.

Focusing on anything but the issue I am reporting does not help me. Even suggesting I exercise and measure my heart rate. Telling me the human heart rate is constantly changing. Literally, answers like they were talking to a small child.

I had high hopes for this device. It disappointed on two levels - Heart Rate, Support. I cannot recommend. Look for a different smart watch if you need accurate heart rate measurements.

reviews.title: NOT AS DESCRIBED AT ALL
reviews.text: I carefully read all reviews before purchasing this smart watch but was so disappointed. It would not hold preferences & kept defaulting to kg & cm. Would not toggle from one thing to another on app. Had to go all the way out of app & go back in before it would toggle from steps to profile to anything else. Sometimes I'd get my cell to ring on my watch, never could answer it or text back from the watch. I'd rather pay the extra & get an apple watch that actually works. I can't tell you how many times I set the preferences & saved them only to find it defaulted right back the next day.  One of the sorriest watches I've ever owned & I kept it 2 weeks before returning it really trying to make it work.

reviews.title: Better than name brand. Long battery life!!
reviews.text: I will take this watch over a fit bit any day! In fact my husband bought me a fit bit and I returned it because thus watch is soooo much better. What I like most is the BATTERY LIFE IS AMAZING! I bought it weeks ago and have only charged it twice.

reviews.title: Never dies

reviews.title: Sleep function is worthless
reviews.text: I really like this watch, has everything I wanted and everything works with one exception:  the sleep analysis feature is worthless.  It only records sleep between 10:00 pm and 8:00 am and if you move or get up it screws up everything. Good customer service, they really try to help, but they will tell you if you get up to go to the bathroom, watch begins recording all over again. Too bad!

reviews.title: My Approach S6 -- Near Perfection!
reviews.text: Very pleased with the quality of this band replacement for my Approach S6 band and the ease of installation. Having a buddy helps, but the installation is easily accomplished on your own within a matter of minutes.  The new band is more flexible than the original which, IMHO, is a plus.  What I can't say is that the original band might have stiffened with age.  Who knows and it doesn't matter.  The screwdrivers are also an awesome design -- the handle ends have a hex shape you can stick in your drill if your hands are weak.  The only minuscule minus is that the retainer bar/strap is a tad large as others have stated, but problem solved by reusing the retainer strap from the original band. VERY pleased with this purchase.

reviews.title: Beautiful Smart Watch
reviews.text: Very pleased.  Handsome titanium case.  Looks more like a  traditional watch than a smart watch.  All functions work on my IPhone 13.  Great choice of faces, including several analog choices.  Always on display is great,  Only one semi-negative.  You must have patience during the pairing process.  Same thing true with programming.  But once it is done, could not ask more from a reasonably priced smart watch. No help from a basically useless manual.  You are on your own.  But, with patience, worth the time it takes to figure out how to pair and operate  Took me about an hour.  Highly recommended

reviews.title: Battery life runs out quickly.
reviews.text: Nice looking.  Great apps and tracking. But battery life isn't all that great.

reviews.title: A good start but needs a lot of work.
reviews.text:
This is a tough review to write. If I could do half stars, this watch would be a 3.5 for me. It does so much right but it feels incomplete and overpriced to me.

First off, the screen and overall build quality is amazing. I have a Galaxy Active 2 as well and, candidly, this Zepp watch is better in pretty much every way in terms of build. The glass feels better and more scratch resistant. You get a stainless frame instead of aluminum. The shocker is the screen. I am not sure how but this watch looks brighter and crisper to me. The size is really nice as well. The Zepp E has a lower profile and almost no visible bezel. If hardware was the lone consideration, this would get a near perfect score.

The battery life is also really outstanding. I get 5 to 7 days with this. It has a proprietary magnetic charger that works well. Even playing with it often and tracking regular workouts doesn't seem to bother it.

I also like Zepp's approach to overall health measurement. The PAI (Personal Activity Intelligence) score tries to give you an overall measurement of how healthy your days are. Once you understand it, I think it is a good general snap shot. If everyone tracked their life like this, the world would be a much healthier place. Sleep tracking is good as well. In line with what I have gotten from Fitbit and Samsung though you need to make sure to set a daily screen timer as it doesn't automatically know to turn off the screen when you are sleeping like the others.

From here though things begin to slip. At $250, there is no onboard GPS. This is a big omission at this price. There also isn't auto tracking for workouts like most others have so you have to remember to physically start tracking workouts. There is no onboard music player though you can control your music from the watch. Interacting with your notifications and inbound calls are all but worthless. Zepp's app is also a mess. It is cumbersome and not well thought out. Everything you want is there but it often takes alot of effort to get from one thing to another. Samsung's app is vastly superior. Lastly, the watch comes with a leather strap. At the time of this writing, Zepp has silicone straps listed on their site but they don't appear to be available yet. A watch that is focused on health should come default with a silicone strap.

I guess I am just not sure what Zepp set out to accomplish with this watch. Is it a smart watch? Not really. Is it a fitness watch? Kind of. Its like they tried to split the baby and ended up accomplishing neither successfully. If you want a good looking everyday digital semi-smart watch with a great battery life, this is worth considering but, for most, at $250, there are better options out there. Perhaps Zepp will update this product and make it more well rounded. If not, I would suggest waiting for a price reduction before investing.

reviews.title: Only thing missing is the talk and messaging
reviews.text: Otherwise, it is similar to Apple watch. I easily set this up and downloaded the software from the app store. It installed easily with absolutely zero issues and you know it is always the software that has glitches. This watch is probably one of the better health smart watches on the market.. It looks nice, it performs nice and has lots of features. You will love the battery life, especially if you have a smartwatch that has to be charged almost daily or maybe ever day and a half. The watch monitors sleep stages, heart rate and 11 different exercises, including swimming. And, it is nice looking. Pretty sweet. I wanted to add that you can select so many faces for your watch without extra charges.

reviews.title: Broke 3 weeks after purchasing
reviews.text: To be fair with the manufacturer, one does not expect for your watch to fall often. In this case, the only time it fell, BOOM - the screen broke on one corner and the display became unusable.

reviews.text:
I wanted a simple activity tracker and this looked like it was perfect. There are a number of things that worked well, battery life, simplicity, matched my needs... However, the accuracy is non existent. I was at the gym working out vigorously and my heart rate was 50 and dropping. I checked it with another app on my phone and I was approaching 140. When I am at rest it creeps into the 90's. by the way, I have entered all profile info. The steps are also way off as I tested against an app on my phone.

I tried but its not worth the effort.

features: WHY APPLE WATCH SE — All the essentials to help you monitor your fitness, keep connected, track your health, and stay safe. Now up to 20 percent faster, with features like Crash Detection and enhanced workout metrics, it’s a better value than ever.
EASILY CUSTOMIZABLE — Available in a range of sizes and colors, with dozens of bands to choose from and watch faces with complications tailored to whatever you’re into.

HEALTH AND SAFETY FEATURES — Get help when you need it with Crash Detection, Fall Detection, and Emergency SOS. Get deep insights into your health, including notifications if you have an irregular rhythm or an unusually high or low heart rate.

SIMPLY COMPATIBLE — It works seamlessly with your Apple devices and services. Unlock your Mac automatically. Find your devices with a tap. Pay and send money with Apple Pay. Apple Watch requires an iPhone 8 or later with the latest iOS version.
SWIMPROOF AND STYLISH — Water resistant to 50 meters. Three finishes. And a redesigned, color-matched back case made with a new production process that reduces its carbon emissions over 80 percent.
APPS ON YOUR WRIST — With access to tens of thousands of apps on the App Store right on your wrist, Apple Watch is the perfect personal device for whatever you love to do.

A POWERFUL FITNESS PARTNER — The enhanced Workout app gives you new ways to train and advanced metrics for more information about your workout performance. And Apple Watch comes with three months of Apple Fitness+ free.
STAY CONNECTED — Send a text, make a call, listen to music and podcasts, use Siri, or call for help with Emergency SOS. Apple Watch SE (GPS) works with your iPhone or Wi-Fi to keep you connected.

APPLECARE — Every Apple Watch comes with one year of hardware repair coverage through its limited warranty and up to 90 days of complimentary technical support. Get AppleCare+ to extend your coverage and reduce the stress and cost of unexpected repairs.
LEGAL DISCLAIMERS — This is a summary of the main product features. See below to learn more.

reviews.title: Some Good Aspects, But Several Annoying Issues
reviews.text:
This CNPGD Smart Watch Fitness Tracker had the potential to be really great. In terms of appearance, the body of the watch combined with the band are sleek and attractive. The watch is available in 4 different color schemes (Black, Pink, Red, and Blue/Silver) and each looks aesthetically pleasing. I received the Black one and really liked the clean lines and modern design. The band fits around the wrist comfortably as well.

Before trying to turn the watch on, I decided to charge it fully overnight. I followed the instructions and photos on the instruction sheet and tried to remove the band from the watch's charging port end. Unfortunately, it was so difficult to remove that I thought I was going to damage the watch or the band at some point as I worked up a sweat trying to pry/pull it apart. I finally managed to insert the lip of a plastic spoon into a sliver of space I had gotten between the band and the watch. I kept working at it until I was finally able to get the band off. I really do not look forward to repeating this process.

Once the watch was charged, I downloaded the WearFit 2.0 App and after a couple of tries, was able to pair my phone with the watch. There are some annoying aspects about the App. In one area, I was able to specify customary units such as pounds and feet versus the default kilograms and centimeters, but in another area, I was unable to make the change. When using the watch, as I started to toggle through the functions, I realized that there was no way to toggle backwards. So if I swiped past my desired function such as the stopwatch feature, I needed to swipe past all the other functions again until I got back to the stopwatch feature. Swiping right or left or pressing the Home button does the same thing and only moves the choice of functions in one direction, forward.

I went out for a long walk and compared the watch's step and distance counter against my phone's fitness app with GPS and a very accurate pocket pedometer. After the walk, my phone fitness app, pedometer, and this watch all gave roughly the same measurement for the mileage. Unfortunately, the step count of the watch was about 2,000 steps off from the other two devices. it's unfortunate that this watch has so many annoying issues as the features such as the BP, O2, and other health trackers are quite handy. I would give this fitness tracker watch more stars if they just made some upgrades/updates to the app and maybe the watch's firmware.

title: Garmin Forerunner 245, GPS Running Smartwatch with Advanced Dynamics, Berry
description: You do the running. Forerunner 245 does the thinking. This GPS smartwatch doesn’t just tell you where you ran. It tracks your stats, crunches the numbers and gets to know all about your performance, your running form, your training history and even your goals. All you need to focus on is putting one foot in front of the other.Bezel Material: fiber-reinforced polymer.
features: GPS running smartwatch with advanced training features
Battery life: up to 7 days in smartwatch mode and up to 24 hours in GPS mode
Get free adaptive training plans from Garmin Coach, or create your own custom workouts on our Garmin Connect online fitness community

Provides advanced running dynamics, including ground contact time balance, stride length, vertical ratio and more (When used with Running Dynamics Pod or HRM-Run or HRM-Tri monitors (sold separately))
Safety and tracking features include incident detection (during select activities) which sends your real-time location to emergency contacts through your paired compatible smartphone
Customize with free watch faces, apps and more from our Connect IQ store
Evaluates your current training status to indicate if you’re undertraining or overdoing it; offers additional performance monitoring features

reviews.title: great looking watch and great for running and biking
reviews.text:
I really like this watch. I replaced and old forerunner. (many generation old) It feels great and looks good since you can download and tailor the watch faces. I have downloaded music to it using MediaMonkey and it worked fine. So far, synching with earbuds Jaybird Tarah Pro and Vistas, has been good. The availability of watch faces and activity widgets is nice.
But
Be warned, it takes a little bit to set it up and understand how to understand how to download and update activity widgets. Updating of watch faces isn't too bad. Be patient and read the user guide, there is a lot of functionality you should be aware of whether you use them or not.

reviews.title: Great purchase
reviews.text:
Great, does all of the stuff running nerds love. Spotify is a hassle to sync when i want to adjust my playlists. This is especially annoying for podcast listeners such as myself. For this reason, I could have gone without the music upgrade and just used my phone but I still use the music feature a good amount for my shorter speed runs where I need the running jams!

But obviously I didn’t take a star because Garmin is the GOAT

reviews.title: Great watch
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband, an avid runner, for our anniversary. He is super into tracking, numbers, stats, etc. And he loves this watch! He previously had the Forerunner 235 Garmin but it was probably close to five years old. He had done alot of research on this one verses the newer models and this one still came out ahead and did not disappoint!

reviews.title: great fitness watch!
reviews.text: if you are looking for a smart watch, then this is not for you. however, if you want a fitness watch, then get this one! the gps tracking is great and accurate! it takes a little bit to get used to the buttons and where all the settings are, but i love that i can customize it to do just what i want in terms of display, audio outputs, etc. the band is comfortable and the watch face is a good size to see my stats while working out without being overly cumbersome (i'm a small 5'0" female). i love that it can play my spotify playlists! the only thing of note is that it will not be able to get any data such as weather, sms notifications, calendar updates, emails, phone calls, or send emergency assistance notices unless you have your phone with you and connected via bluetooth. the point of this watch for me was to keep my phone at home so those features don't work during my run, but it does do the fitness tracking and play previously setup spotify playlists, so it's exactly what i want and need. i don't use it for sleep tracking since i only wear it during workouts. love the heart rate monitoring too!

reviews.title: Sleep Tracking and Incoming Call Notification (on android) are EPIC failures
reviews.text:
Overwhelming 5 start reviews really make me skeptical that the reviews themselves are genuine....
Overall the watch is not bad but really needs functional work. The Android Dev/QA team should have some re-training/structuring after releasing this to the general public...
Sleep tracking shows me as sleeping if I am in bed relaxed watching tv during sleep hours, making it useless. I only receive an incoming call notification on the watch if my phone screen is unlocked...
missed calls come in every time..

no problems with GPS, Battery Life, Music , Bluetooth, feeds to 3rd party athletic tracking apps. all those work fine.

Not sure I would buy this again.

reviews.title: Very limited life span
reviews.text: Too costly for what is now a disposable watch.  Within about 3 months the watch started randomly sending two vibrating signals like it was trying to make a notification of some sort.  Second vibration came with a short screen request to shut down the watch.  Side button quit working after about a month but was not an issue.  Still kept accurate time, fairly good step counts, and notifications of incoming texts and phone calls.  Just can’t stand the frequent and random vibrating. Unlike other commenters my watches battery life was very good.

reviews.title: It stopped for no reason twice
reviews.text: I am returning the first one that I got because I think perhaps there may be a flaw in it because for no reason at all it just shut itself off or wouldn't show me any information on the watch and it did that to me twice so when it happened again today the second time I decided I'm returning it and requesting a replacement if it happens with the replacement then I'm getting my refund. I don't like sleeping with it on it's uncomfortable not used to sleeping with any kind of jewelry I did use it a few days and checked my sleep and it seemed to give me the same answer every time so I don't know if that's very accurate either? I realize these gadgets are not accurate however I'm just trying to find a way to motivate myself to move around more and I refuse to pay over $100 for one of these smart watches so I got this one on a lightning deal.

reviews.title: Pretty much a Fenix 6S with a better battery
reviews.text:
I figured I'd give my 6S to the wife and try the 7S Sapphire Solar. In general the watches are nearly identical, but the 7S does have a noticably better battery. When we go hiking, we get identical information, the 7S doesn't need L5 GPS but it works fine. I like the addition of the touch display. Lastly, Garmin is finally getting their act together on software, the 6S and 7S both work well with Garmin Golf and Connect, lots of good data and charts.

There are a few more data pages on the watch for me to look at than on the 6S. I suspect the 6S could have them too but Garmin wants you to upgrade.

Bottom line, if you have a 6, wait for the 8, maybe it will have ECG?

title: Polar Vantage V2 - Premium Multisport Smartwatch with GPS
features: Premium Sleek Design for superior look and feel provides you with a high-performing, lightweight (only 52g) sports watch that won’t weigh you down whilst you train.
Ultra-Long Battery Life - up to 40h of training with full GPS and HR tracking (and even up to 100h with power save options). Up to 7 days in watch mode with 24/7 HR tracking.
Our World-Renowned Optical HR Technology with 10 LED’s for deeper light penetration, and 4 detectors to sense light returning through the blood vessels to track your HR with precision.

The Training Load Pro and Recovery tracking tools help you optimize your training and avoid overtraining. Test your performance and personalize your heart rate, speed and power zones with the running and cycling performance tests.

Fully Automatic and Intelligent Overnight Recovery Measurement applications give you daily feedback on how well you recover from stress and training during the night. This helps you make optimal choices the next day.

reviews.title: Good watch, I recommend
reviews.text: I came from a fitbit for long years and moved to Polar because of stats and data that I wanted! I started off with the Polar Unite since November 2020 and until now I upgraded to the Polar Vantage V2! It's worth it! I love the accuracy of polar. Heart rate, sleep and GPS is super good!

title: Garmin Venu Sq Music, GPS Smartwatch with Bright Touchscreen Display, Features Music and Up to 6 Days of Battery Life, Slate and Moss Green
description: Now is the perfect time to move. Featuring a bright color display, the Venu Sq – Music Edition GPS smartwatch combines daily style and on-device music storage with health monitoring and fitness features that inspire you to keep moving. Estimate your heart rate, sleep, stress, Body Battery energy levels and so much more. Walk, run, ride or choose from more than 20 preloaded sports apps for indoor or outdoor activities. Never miss a call or text with smart notifications delivered to your wrist (when paired with a compatible smartphone). Download music for phone-free listening, and breeze through checkout lines and select transit systems with Garmin Pay contactless payments (with a supported payment network). Go longer between charges with up to 6 days of battery life. See everything clearly on a bright color display that includes an always-on mode, perfect for quick glances.
features: Fits wrists with a circumference of 125-190 mm
Get an uninterrupted picture of your health with up to 6 days of battery life in smartwatch mode
Go longer with up to 6 hours of battery life in GPS mode with music

Easily download songs to your watch, including playlists from Spotify, Amazon Music or Deezer accounts (requires app on a compatible smartphone), and connect with wireless headphones (sold separately) for phone-free listening
Find new ways to keep moving with more than 20 preloaded sports apps, including walking, running, cycling, mindful breathing, swimming, golf and many more
Use preloaded workouts that include cardio, yoga, strength and even Pilates, create your own in the Garmin Connect app (when paired with a compatible smartphone)
Leave your cash and cards at home; Garmin Pay contactless payments (with a supported payment network) let you pay for purchases on the go

Safety and tracking features include incident detection (during select activities) and notification, both of which send your real-time location to designated contacts (when paired with a compatible smartphone)
See everything clearly on a bright color display that includes an always-on mode, perfect for quick glances
Health is important to you, so monitor everything from your Body Battery energy levels, respiration, hydration and stress to sleep, your menstrual cycle, estimated heart rate and more

reviews.title: Love this watch
reviews.text: Nice looking watch with accurate steps. I love the gps feature where it shows your walk on a true map on the app. Nice visual. Heart rate is very accurate as well. Haven't used music feature as much as I thought I would. Garmin makes a nice watch. Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Heart rate way off
reviews.text:
The main thing I bought this for is to monitor heart rate while hiking. Its reading is way off for me - as confirmed by a pulse ox and manually counting. The watch kept telling me my heart rate was over 100 just around the house. I have nsvt but my heart rate generally runs low. So I'm very sensitive when I have palpitations - when this watch kept saying my heart rate was over 95 I wasnt buying it. That brings into question any other readings or parameters.

I find the watch annoying and frustrating to use. The watch face does not interact very well for me. I'm constantly trying to tap it and it wont register so then the screen shuts off. Another annoying thing is it tells you to start moving around when I'm doing housework. I guess you can shut that off but I've really lost interest. Hopefully Garmin can offer some fix for the reading since its past the return date.

The positives - watch is comfortable and I don't wear jewelry. I forget its on. Its a large size so its readable if you have vision issues. I've had no problem with that.

reviews.title: Lightweight and perfect for small wrists
reviews.text: This is a great lightweight fitness tracker.  It has about a 4 day battery life and does a quick recharge.  It has a little learning curve for the activity tracking but it is right up there with my Apple Watch.

reviews.title: Exelente
reviews.text: La bateria me dura 14 dias. Pantalla se ve perfectamente bajo el sol. La app connect trabaja muy bien, en la tienda puedo descargar pantallas para el reloj. Resto de las actividades ok. Lo recomiendo

reviews.title: Large Screen-Bright White Band
reviews.text: I like everything about this except that I didn’t realize that the frame and the band would be a Brilliant white! There is a golden strip surrounding the watch face.
